methods.description: The phylogenetic analysis aims to assess the phylogenetic position of a new specimen using a modified data matrix from previous studies. This analysis helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships among different taxa within the Rhinocerotidae family.

steps.description: The phylogenetic analysis in this paper is performed using a modified data matrix from Antoine (2002) and Antoine (2003) to assess the phylogenetic position of the new specimen which can be found in the Appendix section.
steps.searchable_summary: use modified data matrix from Antoine (2002, 2003) for phylogenetic analysis

steps.description: A heuristic search was used to perform the phylogenetic analysis using PAUP4.0a169(Swofford, 2002), with TBR, 1,000 replications with random addition sequence, 10 trees held at each step, treating gaps as missing, and no differential weighting or topological constraint a priori.
steps.searchable_summary: perform heuristic search using PAUP4.0a169 with TBR and 1,000 replications

steps.description: To explore the phylogenetic relationships of the Tongxin specimen, we performed a phylogenetic analysis of the Rhinocerotidae (based on the data matrix of Antoine (2002); Antoine (2003), with the addition of several new taxa, resulting in four equally most parsimonious trees.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze phylogenetic relationships with additional taxa to find most parsimonious trees

steps.description: All members of Teleoceratina are clustered in a single clade (Node A) (Fig. 4).
steps.searchable_summary: visualize clade clustering in phylogenetic tree

steps.description: The clade including the plesiaceratheres (Node B; Fig. 4) is supported by twenty unequivocal synapomorphies.
The new species Plesiaceratherium tongxinense established here is located in the basal position of the genus Plesiaceratherium supported by twenty-one unambiguous synapomorphies.
steps.searchable_summary: determine basal position of new species using synapomorphies
